clerk.def: 辦事員、職員、店員、擔任職員的人的人物 (單數)
clerk.col: bank clerk, town clerk, law clerk
clerk.ex: The bank clerk helped her to open a new savings account.

clerkship.def: 書記職務、實習期人物 (單數)
clerkship.ex: He completed a year-long judicial clerkship in the city.

clever.def: 聰明的、靈巧的、機敏的人的人物
clever.col: clever idea, clever person, too clever by half
clever.ex: That was a very clever solution to a difficult problem.

cleverly.ex: The entrance was disguised cleverly as a large bookshelf.

cleverness.ex: Her natural cleverness helped her to succeed in business.

clevis.ex: A strong steel clevis was used to attach the heavy trailer.

clew.def: 線團、(古)線索的人物 (單數)
clew.ex: She held a clew of thread as she moved through the cave.

cliche/cliché.def: 陳腔濫調、老套的人物 (單數)
cliche/cliché.ex: The movie script was full of tired and boring old cliches.

click.def: 咔嗒聲、點擊、使發出咔嗒聲的人物 (單數)
click.col: double click, mouse click, click on
click.ex: You need to double click on the icon to open the file.

clicking.def: 正在發出咔嗒聲的的人 (或物)
clicking.ex: The clicking noise of the heater bothered me all night.

client.def: 委託人、客戶、顧客的人物 (單數)
client.col: regular client, important client, legal client
client.ex: The lawyer met with an important regular client today.

clientele.def: 委託人(總稱)、顧客、客戶群的人物 (單數)
clientele.ex: The luxury hotel attracts a very wealthy international clientele.

cliff.col: white cliff, sea cliff, cliff edge
cliff.ex: White cliffs rise high above the deep and cold blue sea.

clifford.def: 克利福德(男子名)人物 (單數)
clifford.ex: Sir Clifford was a well-known figure in local politics.

cliffhanger.def: 懸念、扣人心弦的結尾人物 (單數)
cliffhanger.ex: The exciting final episode ended on a dramatic cliffhanger.

climatological.ex: Scientists are analyzing the latest climatological data.

climatology.ex: Climatology is a vital field for understanding global warming.

climax.def: 高潮、頂點、達到高潮的人的人
climax.ex: The movie reached its dramatic climax in the final scene.

climb.def: 攀爬、上升、爬升、爬的人的人
climb.col: climb up, social climb, steep climb
climb.ex: It was a very long and steep climb to the mountain top.

climber.def: 登山者、攀登者的人物 (單數)
climber.ex: The mountain climber reached the high peak before sunset.

climbing.ex: Mountain climbing can be a very dangerous and hard sport.

clinch.def: 確定、達成(協議)、互相緊抱的人的人
clinch.ex: They managed to clinch a deal after weeks of talk today.

clincher.def: 決定性因素、敲定者的人物 (單數)
clincher.ex: The low price was the final clincher for the buyers.

cling.def: 黏著、依附、堅持的人的人
cling.col: cling to, cling together, clingy fabric
cling.ex: The wet shirt began to cling to his cold and tired skin.

clingy.def: 黏人的、緊身的的人的人物
clingy.ex: She has a very clingy child who won't let her go tonight.

clinic.def: 診所、門診部的人物 (單數)
clinic.col: private clinic, health clinic, eye clinic
clinic.ex: He went to the local health clinic for a quick checkup.

clinical.def: 臨床的、診所的、冷靜克制的人的人物
clinical.col: clinical trial, clinical depression, clinical psychologist
clinical.ex: The new drug is currently undergoing advanced clinical trials.

clinically.def: 臨床地、冷靜地、診斷上地
clinically.ex: The patient was pronounced dead clinically by doctors.

clinician.ex: He is a very skilled clinician with years of experience.

clink.def: 叮噹聲、(俚)牢房、發出叮鐺聲的人的人物
clink.ex: We heard the clink of glasses from the next local room.

clinker.def: 煤渣、熔渣、(俚)大錯的人物 (單數)
clinker.ex: Hard clinker from the furnace was used to pave the road.

clinking.def: 叮噹響的、發鏗鏘聲的人 (或物)
clinking.ex: I could hear the clinking chains of the ghost all night.

clip.def: 夾子、迴紋針、修剪、電影片段的人物 (單數)
clip.col: paper clip, hair clip, movie clip, clip off
clip.ex: She used a small hair clip to keep her long hair back.

clipboard.def: 剪貼板、中轉站人物 (單數)
clipboard.ex: Please copy the email address to your computer clipboard.

clique.def: 派系、小集團的人物 (單數)
clique.ex: An exclusive clique of students controlled the local club.

cliquish.def: 派系的、排他的的人人物
cliquish.ex: The office environment was very cliquish and unfriendly.

clitoris.ex: The clitoris is an important part of the female anatomy.

cloak.def: 斗篷、披風、掩蓋、掩飾的人的人物
cloak.col: cloak of secrecy, under the cloak of night
cloak.ex: They escaped from the city under the heavy cloak of night.

cloakroom.def: 寄物處、衣帽間、(英)洗手間的人物 (單數)
cloakroom.ex: Please leave your heavy coats in the hotel cloakroom.

clobber.def: (口)痛毆、擊敗、衣服的人的人物 (單數)
clobber.ex: The local team managed to clobber their rivals yesterday.

cloche.def: 鐘形女帽、罩、鐘形蓋的人物 (單數)
cloche.ex: Use a garden cloche to protect young plants from frost.

clock.col: around the clock, o'clock, face of the clock
clock.ex: The old wooden clock in the hall struck twelve tonight.

clockmaker.ex: The master clockmaker spent months fixing the big clock.

clockwise.def: 順時針地、順時針方向的人的人物 (順)
clockwise.ex: Please rotate the large metal dial in a clockwise direction.

clockwork.def: 發條裝置、機械的人的人物 (單數)
clockwork.ex: Everything went like clockwork and we finished early today.

clod.def: 土塊、笨蛋、肉塊的人物 (單數)
clod.ex: He threw a large and heavy clod of earth at the target.

clog.def: 木鞋、障礙、阻塞、妨礙的人的人
clog.ex: Too much fat in your diet can clog up your heart arteries.

clogging.ex: Hair and soap were clogging up the local bathroom pipes.

cloister.def: 迴廊、修道院、禁閉的人的人
cloister.ex: The monks walked quietly around the shadowy cloister.

cloistered.def: 隱居的、與世隔絕的人的人 (或物)
cloistered.ex: She lived a very cloistered life in a remote monastery.

clone.def: 複製、克隆、酷似的人的人的人
clone.ex: The scientists managed to clone a sheep successfully.

cloning.ex: The ethics of human cloning are still being discussed.

close.def: 關閉、接近、親密的、結束的人的人物
close.col: close to, close down, close at hand, close friend
close.ex: Please close the window because it is getting cold out.

closed.def: 關閉了的、封閉的人的人 (或物)
closed.ex: The local library is closed on Sunday mornings today.

closed-circuit.def: 閉路的人、閉合電路的人的人物
closed-circuit.ex: The store is protected by a new closed-circuit camera.

closely.ex: The police are watching the local suspect very closely.

closeness.def: 接近、親密、秘密的人物 (單數)
closeness.ex: There was a great feeling of closeness in the family.

closet.def: 壁櫥、廁所、小房間、私下的人的人的人物
closet.col: in the closet (不公開身分), water closet (WC)
closet.ex: She hung her new expensive winter coat in the closet.
